title: Add Phase 1 correctness test suite
description: Add concurrency, crash recovery, idempotency, blocked-work, and state-machine tests for the execution core.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove no duplicate claims, expired leases return eligible work, blocked tickets are not claimed until resolved, terminal attempts reject invalid transitions, and claim idempotency replays correctly.

title: Build JSON-first Phase 1 CLI
description: Build core CLI commands over the same domain/API semantics.
acceptance_criteria: CLI supports create, propose, claim-next, heartbeat, checkpoint, complete, fail, block, list, get; --json output is stable; commands have predictable exit codes.

title: Expose Phase 1 Huma REST API
description: Expose core execution operations through REST using Huma and OpenAPI generation.
acceptance_criteria: REST routes exist for tickets, propose, ready, claim-next, attempts, heartbeat, checkpoint, complete, fail, block, cancel, events, and basic artifact registration; OpenAPI is generated.

title: Add River workers for lease expiry and cleanup
description: Add Postgres-backed background workers for stale lease expiry and idempotency cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: worker expires stale running attempts, updates tickets according to retry policy, writes events, and cleans expired idempotency records.

title: Implement idempotency middleware and claim replay
description: Implement machine-facing idempotency support, with special handling for claim-next response replay after committed claims.
acceptance_criteria: same key and request hash replays stored response; same key with different request conflicts; claim-next stores ticket_id/attempt_id/context response in the claim transaction.

title: Implement attempt completion, failure, block, cancel, and expiry transitions
description: Implement explicit ticket and attempt state machine transitions for terminal and blocked outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: complete/fail/block/cancel/expire enforce valid transitions; blocked work is distinct from failure; ticket statuses update per retry/review/block policy; events are written transactionally.

title: Implement transactional claim-next with context bundle
description: Implement Postgres-owned claim-next semantics with eligibility filtering and a claim context bundle returned to the caller.
acceptance_criteria: claim-next filters by workspace/project/status/type/tags/harness/capabilities/dependencies/retry eligibility; creates attempt and claimed event in one transaction; returns ticket, attempt, and context bundle.

title: Implement attempt heartbeat and checkpoint
description: Implement cheap heartbeats for liveness and durable checkpoints for resumable progress.
acceptance_criteria: heartbeat extends lease and writes heartbeat event; checkpoint updates attempt summary/next step/progress, creates checkpoint row, and writes checkpointed event.

title: Implement ticket creation, proposal, validation, and listing
description: Implement domain/service behavior for creating tickets, proposing backlog tickets, validating ticket quality, and listing tickets.
acceptance_criteria: Human-created and agent-created tickets are supported; agent-created tickets default to backlog unless enqueue is permitted; validation checks title, project, type, acceptance criteria, and useful context.

title: Generate sqlc models and core queries
description: Add sqlc configuration, handwritten SQL queries, and generated Go types for core Phase 1 tables.
acceptance_criteria: sqlc generation succeeds; queries cover create/list/get tickets, dependencies, attempts, checkpoints, events, idempotency, and metrics basics.

title: Create core Postgres migrations
description: Add migrations for workspaces, projects, tickets, ticket dependencies, attempts, attempt checkpoints, events, idempotency keys, API keys, and attempt metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Migrations apply cleanly to empty Postgres; schema represents PRD Phase 1 fields; rollback strategy is documented or intentionally omitted for initial development.

title: Add configuration and process boot
description: Add configuration loading and process startup plumbing for API server, worker process, and CLI commands.
acceptance_criteria: Configuration supports env/config file defaults; server and worker can start with clear validation errors; tests cover config parsing.

title: Bootstrap Go monolith skeleton
description: Create the initial Forge Go project structure and bootable binary shape for server, worker, mcp, tui, and CLI command modes.
acceptance_criteria: go.mod exists; cmd/forge/main.go boots; internal package layout matches PRD; forge --help works; no product behavior is implemented beyond command skeletons.
close_reason: Completed Go monolith skeleton with command help, package layout, tests, and build verification.

title: Phase 1: Execution Core
description: Build the correctness-first Forge execution ledger: schema, transactional claim-next, attempts, events, checkpoints, idempotency, REST, CLI, workers, and verification.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 supports the day-zero flow from PRD.md: create ticket, claim with context, heartbeat/checkpoint, complete/fail/block, write events, replay claim idempotency, and prove concurrency/lease correctness.

title: Document Phase 1 local development and day-zero flow
description: Document setup, migrations, server/worker/CLI usage, and the PRD day-zero flow for developers and future agents.
acceptance_criteria: README or docs explain how to run Postgres, apply migrations, start services, run CLI commands, execute tests, and walk through create->claim->checkpoint->complete/fail/block.

title: Add proof artifact registration for Phase 1
description: Add lightweight artifact metadata registration for diffs, test output, final responses, diagnostics, and handoff evidence without full storage abstraction yet.
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts can be registered against tickets/attempts with type and role; claim context and detail views can retrieve registered proof metadata.

title: Phase 3: Human Operations
description: Build fast, beautiful, low-friction TUI and web surfaces for developers to inspect, steer, and trust agent work.
acceptance_criteria: Developers can inspect queues, tickets, attempts, checkpoints, blockers, artifacts, proposed work, and execution activity through polished TUI and web views.

title: Phase 2: Agent Integration
description: Make Forge easy for real agents and harnesses to use through MCP, capabilities, templates, Codex convenience commands, and parity tests.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can use REST, CLI, or MCP with equivalent semantics; Codex convenience commands exist; agent-created work follows templates and validation.

title: Phase 4: Artifacts, Search, and Analytics
description: Make Forge useful as execution history through artifact storage, full-text search, metrics, summaries, and webhooks.
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts, search, and analytics expose proof and historical execution context across projects, agents, harnesses, and attempts.

title: Phase 5: Intelligence and Advanced Coordination
description: Add semantic search, historical similarity, recommendations, policy, advanced RBAC, and optional realtime/observability integrations after real usage exists.
acceptance_criteria: Advanced coordination features are added only after the core ledger has real usage data and remains low-ceremony.
